title: cleistocactus acanthurus subsp. canetensis
intro: Cleistocactus acanthurus subsp. canetensis is a rare and endemic species of cactus, native to the Canete Province in Peru. It belongs to the cactus family (Cactaceae) and is prized for its vibrant yellow flowers and unique, cylindrical shape. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides. Cleistocactus acanthurus subsp. canetensis is used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
